title: Solutions Architect - Epicor ERP - Canada
description: Solution Architect (Epicor ERP) Department: Development Team Company Overview Six S Partners is a recognized leader in Epicor ERP implementations, customizations, and management consulting. As Epicor’s “Global Partner of the Year” multiple years and a “Certified Platinum Partner,” we deliver tailored ERP solutions to mid-market companies worldwide. Role Summary The Solution Architect is part of Six S Partners’ Development Team and acts as the key link between our customers and our developers. You will lead discovery and scoping conversations, determine whether requirements can be met through Epicor’s standard functionality or require customization, and translate outcomes into clear design documents and user stories. You’ll also own validation (testing delivered work against the design) then produce the customer-facing documentation that explains what was built and how to use it. If you love Epicor, enjoy solving complex problems, working directly with customers, and take pride in reading and writing excellent documentation, this role will fit you well. Key Outcomes •Customer needs are clearly defined, validated, and translated into build-ready requirements •The right approach is chosen (Epicor standard vs customization) and documented •Developers have unambiguous user stories and acceptance criteria to build from •Delivered work is thoroughly tested and meets expectations before release •Customers receive clear documentation that explains the delivered functionality •Agile delivery stays organized, predictable, and transparent Responsibilities •Lead discovery/scoping calls to understand requirements, constraints, and success criteria •Evaluate Epicor’s out-of-the-box capabilities vs customization; recommend the best path forward •Write high-quality design documents and user stories that customers and developers can both understand •Define acceptance criteria and ensure requirements are testable and complete •Own backlog creation and refinement, sprint planning, and coordination of sprint ceremonies (review, retro, etc.) •Test delivered functionality against the design; provide clear feedback to developers to close gaps •Produce customer-facing documentation (how it works, how to use it, what changed) •Partner closely with developers and internal stakeholders to remove ambiguity and keep delivery moving Skills and Qualifications Must have: •Strong Epicor ERP knowledge, especially operations and/or finance modules (5 years experience) •Hands-on experience with Epicor tools (BAQs, BPMs, Functions, AppStudio) •Excellent writing skills and a genuine interest in reading/writing documentation daily •Strong analytical mindset, able to turn messy requirements into clear, testable user stories •Comfortable leading customer conversations, asking sharp questions, and clarifying scope •Experience running agile delivery (backlog, refinement, sprint planning, story writing) •Strong organizational skills in situations that are fast paced with high workloads. Nice to have •SSRS reporting experience and related Microsoft technologies •C# development experience •Jira experience Why This Role You’ll have real ownership: you help shape what gets built, you validate that it works, and you document it so customers succeed. You’ll work in a collaborative dev environment where good requirements and good documentation are treated as core to quality delivery. Job Type: Full-time Pay: $80,000.00-$90,000.00 per year Experience: Epicor ERP: 5 years (required) Work Location: Remote

title: Advice-Only Financial Planner
description: About Us Merrick Financial Inc. is a premier fee-only, advice-only financial planning firm based in Toronto, serving clients across Canada. We operate on a pure advice model: we never sell financial products like investments, insurance, or mortgages. Our mission is to provide unbiased, high-impact strategies that offer true financial clarity to business owners, professionals, and those planning for retirement. What You’ll Do Drive Business Growth: Use sophisticated sales and communication skills to articulate why our advice-only model is the best option for the client and close the deal. Lead Discovery Consultations: Conduct initial video consultations to uncover client needs, build immediate trust, and demonstrate your professional value. Persistent Lead Management: Take full ownership of the sales pipeline by consistently following up with leads to move them through the planning process. You will be supported by our marketing team’s lead generation efforts to ensure your calendar stays active. Craft Comprehensive Plans: Utilize industry-leading software (Snap Projections or Adviice) and AI tools like Google Gems and Notebooks to build tailored financial plans and scenarios. Deliver High-Impact Presentations: Present complex written financial plans via Google Meets in a way that is professional, personal, and easy for clients to digest. Nurture Relationships: Provide ongoing advice and service to a dedicated portfolio of families and business owners, ensuring their action items stay on track. Maintain Technical Excellence: Research evolving financial planning topics to ensure every recommendation is backed by current data and best practices. What You Bring 3+ Years of Experience: Proven track record in a client-facing financial planning or sales-heavy role. Elite Sales Ability: You are comfortable selling yourself and your advice; you don't rely on a "big brand" logo to establish credibility. Certified Expertise: A CFP® designation is required (or PFP®/working toward CFP). Tech-Forward Mindset: High degree of proficiency in financial planning software and a commitment to being an AI practitioner. Remote Discipline: The ability to work independently from a home office while maintaining high focus and professional standards. Values Alignment: A genuine passion for and belief in the fee-only/advice-only financial planning model. PLEASE NOTE: If you are only interested in technical back-office planning and have no interest in proactive sales, consistent lead follow-up, or the "soft-sell" of professional expertise, this job is not for you. We are looking for advisors who love connecting with people and closing the deal. Why You’ll Love Working Here (Benefits) High Earning Potential: A competitive 60% commission-only structure on gross revenue, with an estimated annual income of $80,000 - $120,000. Guaranteed Start-Up Support: A weekly draw of $1,000 for your first 5 weeks to provide stable income during your ramp-up period. This would be a draw on commission. Steady Lead Flow: We provide an average of 3-4 warm leads per week with initial consultations already booked for you. Fully Remote: Enjoy a flexible schedule while working from anywhere in Canada with full coaching and management support. Professional Tools: Merrick Financial covers your work-related expenses, including essential planning software. Our Hiring Process: Initial Interview: A 30-minute deep dive into your credentials, your philosophy on advice-only planning, and your overall fit for our remote culture. Discovery & Sales Role Play: A mock initial consultation. We want to see how you present yourself, the specific discovery questions you ask to uncover pain points, and—most importantly—how you "close" the client on the value of our advice-only model. Final Values Alignment: A final conversation to discuss long-term goals, compensation structure, and a deep dive into our AI-driven planning tools. The Offer: If you’ve proven you can sell the vision and deliver the expertise, we’ll welcome you to the team. Stop Wasting Time on 'Ghost' Postings: We are actively and urgently growing. With a backlog of high-quality leads waiting for advice, we need a talented Financial Planner to join us now. If you have the sales skills and the CFP® designation, we have the clients ready for you to engage. Job Type: Full-time Pay: $80,000.00-$120,000.00 per year Benefits: Work from home Application question(s): You will receive ~3-4 warm leads per week. What is your process for following up with leads who don't book or commit immediately after an initial consultation? In 2-3 sentences, explain why you believe the "Advice-Only" model is superior to the traditional AUM/Commission model and how you communicate that value to a skeptical lead. Experience: financial planning: 3 years (preferred) Licence/Certification: a CFP designation (or PFP and are working toward your CFP®)? (required) Location: Canada (required) Work Location: Remote

description: Location: Cobalt Refinery, Temiskaming Shores, Ontario Reference No: 2024-027 Position Type: Full-time Position Status: This position is being advertised to fill an existing vacancy. Rate of Pay: Annual salary range of $90,000-$120,000, commensurate with experience, skills, and qualifications. ABOUT ELECTRA BATTERY MATERIALS Electra is a leader in advancing North America’s critical minerals supply chain for lithium-ion batteries. The Company’s primary focus is constructing North America’s only cobalt sulfate refinery, as part of a phased strategy to onshore critical minerals refining and reduce reliance on foreign supply chains. In addition to the Refinery, Electra holds a significant land package in Idaho’s Cobalt Belt, including its Iron Creek project and surrounding properties, positioning the Company as a potential cornerstone for North American cobalt and copper production. Electra is also advancing black mass recycling opportunities to recover critical materials from end-of-life batteries, while continuing to evaluate growth opportunities in nickel refining and other downstream battery materials. RESPONSIBILITIES Below is a list of primary duties and responsibilities for the Cost Controller and Project Scheduler position at the Cobalt Refinery: Support the Finance team in managing the project accounting functions, including maintaining accounting principles, practices and procedures; Develop and maintain the cost accounting system, documents, and records of the project; Use project scheduling software to input tasks and timing for mechanical, electrical, and civil works to generate overall schedule Gantt chart; Prepare cost reports for senior management, advise management of variation to budget and provide estimated costs to complete; Responsible for organization and distribution of payment of invoices; Process and verify invoices in a timely and accurate manner; Classify costs accurately and prepare financial reports; Assist with month end and year end duties as required; Assist in audits and general ledger preparation; Ensure accuracy and compliance to accounting standards and internal control; Providing management and clients with timely financial reports and budgets, as well as project forecasts; Monitoring accounts receivable and payable; Updating contracts according to client requirements and reporting associated budget amendments; Compile information for internal and external auditors, as required; Maintain project-related records, including contracts and change orders; Review and present schedules on a bimonthly basis to site construction team Identify critical path and provide oversight of project schedule; Analyze schedule progression and identify schedule discrepancies and opportunities; Present to senior management as required; and, Other duties as assigned by management. QUALIFICATIONS Post-secondary education in Accounting preferred; Minimum 5 years of related field experience in cost control and project scheduling required; Supporting post-secondary classes on cost control and scheduling considered an asset; Project Management Professional (PMP) certification considered an asset; CPA designation considered an asset; Must be local or willing to relocate to Temiskaming Shores, ON; Experience with electronic accounting systems; Knowledge of scheduling software, namely Primavera (P6); High proficiency of Microsoft Office applications; Proven ability to work in a team environment demonstrating excellent communication skills; Strong analytical skills with attention to detail and accuracy; Experience working in a fast-paced office and deadline oriented; Superior organizational, problem solving and time management skills; and, Excellent written and oral communications. WHY JOIN ELECTRA BATTERY MATERIALS? Together, we will power a better path forward. Be a part of a fast-growing team, as we succeed in becoming North America’s first integrated battery materials park, providing battery grade materials for the electric vehicle revolution. Our success is contingent on the hard work and dedication of our people. Electra Battery Materials rewards team members through: Recognition and results-driven compensation Training and development opportunities Culture where innovation is rewarded Health and wellbeing Ongoing communication and two-way feedback If you are a passionate, committed, and dynamic individual, please submit your resume in confidence to careers@ElectraBMC.com. Please note that only qualified candidates selected for an interview will be contacted. Electra Battery Materials is an equal opportunity employer. We are committed to providing an environment of mutual respect and we believe that diversity and inclusion among our team members is critical to our success. title: Plant Manager (Batcher)
description: Plant Manager (Batcher) United Ready Mix is a rapidly growing ready-mix concrete company supplying concrete across the Greater Toronto Area. We are focused on supplying quality concrete, safely and on time at competitive prices. Position Summary: Reporting to the Sales and Operations Manager, the Plant Manager is responsible for the accurate and efficient batching of ready-mix concrete in accordance with mix designs, production schedules, and the company’s Quality Assurance (QA) Program. The role involves operating the batching system, monitoring material inputs, coordinating with dispatch, drivers, loader operators and maintenance staff, and supporting the delivery of concrete products to customers. Locations: 635031 ON-10, Orangeville, ON L9W 2Z1 Hours of Operations: 6am to 6pm (plus potential night and weekend shifts) Wage rate: $27-$32 per hour (Based on qualifications, experience and relevant skills) Key Responsibilities: Plant: · Operate and monitor the batching plant to produce concrete mixes according to job specifications and established standards. · Ensure proper calibration and accuracy of batching equipment (cement, water, aggregates, admixtures) as per the company’s QA Program. · Adjust mix proportions based on environmental conditions or project requirements (e.g., hot weather, slump requirements) while adhering to the company’s QA Program. · Working with the Loader Operator, coordinate daily activities to manage material inventory and support production requirements, and maintain a clean and organized yard. · Manage boiler operation to maintain aggregate, water and plant heating. Labour: · Supervise hourly labour and coordinate with dispatch, drivers to ensure on-time deliveries, dispatch when required. · Assist operations in the supervision and coordination of the production employees, including non-productive hours, concrete blocks, truck cleaning, etc. · Report any discrepancies or equipment malfunctions to maintenance staff and management ASAP. · Actively promote a strong team environment through team collaboration and communication. Administration: · Maintain batch records, production logs, and required documentation as per the company’s QA Program. · Coordinate C.O.D orders cash management with dispatch and drivers. · Coordinate general administrative activities, including submission of attendance, timesheet records. · Support hiring activities and training for hourly site personnel. Health & Safety, and Environmental · Follow company Health & Safety, and Environmental policies and procedures. · Perform and document daily inspections and routine maintenance as per company policies and procedures. · Ensure that all chemicals and materials are stored and handled in a safe and efficient manner as per WHMIS standards and company policies. · Report workplace incidents, near misses, and accidents which may involve personal injury or property damage as per company policies and procedures. Qualification Requirements: · High school diploma or equivalent required. · Experience in ready mix concrete production and construction industry. · Able to perform heavy lifting >25kg. · Able work both inside and outside, in the heat and cold, and around moving equipment, and at heights above four (4) feet. · Be able to work irregular hours such as nights and weekend work. · Proficient with Microsoft applications, specifically Excel and Word and batch software. Benefits: Competitive Salary with annual cost of living increases Annual Bonus Program 100% Company Paid Benefits Extended Health Care Dental Care Vision Care Disability Insurance Vacation & Personal Days, plus Paid Holiday Shutdown Parental Leave Top-up Program Employee Assistance Program (EAP) Life Insurance Wellness Programs Free On-site Parking AODA: United Ready Mix inc. is committed to maintaining an accessible and inclusive organization. We are committed to providing barrier-free and accessible employment practices in compliance with the Accessibility for Ontarians with Disabilities Act (AODA). Disability-related accommodation during the application process is available upon request. Please, no agencies Job Types: Full-time, Permanent Pay: $27.00-$32.00 per hour Work Location: In person

description: Lumerate is growing rapidly and we’re searching for a Business Development Manager on the Zapyrus brand team to join our team for the journey! Who is Lumerate? Lumerate is a Toronto-based SaaS company that has built game-changing technology to help sales teams accelerate revenue growth. We help our customers achieve the full picture of their industries. We also strive to achieve our own personal full pictures from a career fulfillment and learning perspective. We’re in the business of gathering intelligence about industries and delivering it to the right people within those industries through innovative software interfaces. All About Zapyrus Zapyrus is the MedTech brand within Lumerate. We are an intelligence tool designed for sales professionals in the medical devices space to understand what is happening in their niches. Today MedTech service providers such as CROs, quality, regulatory, design & engineering, and manufacturing houses all over the world trust Zapyrus for their sales research. What you’ll be responsible for: Moving and closing sales opportunities through the sales pipeline Collaborating with our Customer Success team to ensure a smooth transition of the customer relationship and maximizing success of any implementations Keeping an eye out for new companies to add to our target lists Hunting for new access routes into prospect companies Initiating cold outreach to viable prospects through whichever methods you deem to be most effective Providing feedback to the product team about what features you think will lead to increased revenue and improved user retention Providing feedback on relative competitor activity and identify emerging competitors in the market Working with trial accounts to ensure a smooth and fruitful trial / pilot period with the software Maintaining internal records and continually suggesting improvements to any administrative aspects of the sales organization Stepping in as a team player in the company to apply your customer savviness where it is needed Helping develop scripts and messaging that facilitates expansion of our overall sales organization Attending conferences and tradeshows as needed by the company. What we’d love to see in your work history: Experience in complex sales at mid-market or enterprise level At least 5 years of medtech or medical devices sales or life-science industry experience Experience collaborating with Marketing and general understand of marketing tactics and strategy Experience working with a complex commercial tech stack Evidence of consistently achieving or exceeding quota Instances where you’ve acquired new knowledge quickly General knowledge of SaaS companies, and medical devices Experience at start-up or mid-level medtech or medical devices company is preferred Who will be successful in this role?‍ A hunter DNA that’s comfortable owning pipeline generation Someone who enjoys selling into B2B environments with multiple stakeholders An excellent communicator who can effectively convey ideas and emotions via email, phone, video conferencing and in-person interactions Someone who is passionate about building relationships A life-long learner who prioritizes learning and development and strives for growth A strategic thinker who loves solving puzzles Someone that enjoys working in a team setting Someone that values feedback and continuous improvement Someone that's self motivated Someone who is deeply inquisitive An optimist with contagious enthusiasm Someone who is detail oriented, so you’ll notice that we’d like you to mention the keyword you see at these coordinates: 43.63647195342424, -79.44162925589113 in your cover letter Why Lumerate? Fancy perks etc. Help shape the future of a bootstrapped and profitable Canadian tech company. Earn yourself some equity (employee options make up 20% of the value of the company at all times). Be a part of a tightly-knit team with a thriving hybrid culture. Three weeks paid vacation + paid statutory holidays. If based in Toronto, enjoy a dedicated work space at our open-concept office (located in the Junction Triangle) and enjoy the benefits of in-person interactions with colleagues 3 days/week. Upgrade your home office setup with our remote or hybrid stipend. Join us for our annual all-company retreat (past destinations include Iceland, Costa Rica, Portugal, Mexico and Spain)! Earn additional paid vacation days with continued learning ($1000 annual stipend for courses and classes). Take part in our Employee Giving Program (you choose the causes and the company provides the funds). Basic and extended health and dental benefits. Paid and topped-up maternal and parental leave. Location: Remote (Canada-based, Eastern Time Zone only) or hybrid from our laid-back office (located in Toronto's Junction Triangle on Tuesdays, Thursdays, and Fridays). ‍Salary: Base salary: CAD $80K - 90K OTE: CAD $120K - $130K Start Date: As soon as the successful candidate is available. Already envisioning your first day at Lumerate as our next Business Development Manager? Please send in your application with two things: your resume and cover letter. We look forward to hearing from you! Please note that any applications without a cover letter will not be considered. Lumerate is committed to creating a diverse environment and is proud to be an equal opportunity employer. All qualified applicants will receive consideration for employment without regard to race, colour, religion, gender, gender identity or expression, sexual orientation, national origin, genetics, disability, age, or veteran status.
